request.question: Create the IC memo on Darb using the data room and our internal template.

events.text: # IN

VESTMENT COMMITTEE MEMORANDUM
## Darb Logistics Technology ("Darb") – Series B
**Stage:** Screening-stage review. This memo is prepared on an
anonymized screening pack of self-reported, unaudited figures.
Full underlying materials (contracts, det
ailed financials, cap table) are available under NDA once the deal advances past screening.
A full Return Analysis (valuation, sc
enario IRRs, benchmark check) is not possible at this stage because no post-money valuation or cap table has been disclosed; this memo therefore presents a

Screening Outcome against the IC Charter in its place.

---

---

## 2. Executive Summary

- **Mandate fit at screening:
clears the hard criteria.** Sector, ticket size, financing stage, and domicile all f

all inside the Charter's investment criteria (detail in Section 3).
- **Growth and
retention profile.**
ARR was SAR 18.2M in FY2025A and is guided to SAR 29
.5M in FY2026E (62% YoY growth), with gross margin of 68% rising to 71%, and net revenue retention of 118% r
ising to 124%.

events.quote:
Metric FY2025A FY2026E
Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) SAR 18.2M SAR 29.5M
YoY ARR growth — 62%
Gross margin 68% 71%
Net revenue retention (NRR) 118% 124%
Logo count (active customers) 34 52
Mont

events.text: - **Asset-light model.**
The product is deployed as a subscription SaaS layered on top of the customer's existing
fleet, avoiding the asset-heavy economics of an owned-fleet delivery business.

events.quote:
The product is deployed as a subscription SaaS layered on top of the customer's existing fleet,
avoiding the asset-heavy economics of an owned-fleet delivery business.

events.text: - **Return threshold not yet test
able.** The Charter sets a
15% base-case IRR benchmark at entry.

events.quote:
Limit Threshold
Minimum underwritten IRR (hurdle
rate)
15% at entry, base case
Single-name concentration cap 10% of AUM, at cost, per portfolio company
Sector concentration cap
10% of AUM, at co

events.text: No
valuation, ownership stake, or exit assumption is in the screening pack, so the benchmark cannot be

underwritten at this gate.
- **Concentration headroom on single name.** At SAR 40M against
~SAR 2.0bn AUM, the ticket is ~2% of AUM at cost (analyst calculation), comfortably inside the 10% single-name cap;

sector-bucket headroom cannot be confirmed without the current private-book exposure.
- **Diligence gaps to
clear.** Financials are self-reported and unaudited; compliance debt and income ratios and the

four red-flag checks are not evidenced in the pack.

---

**Benchmark /
return check:** deferred.
No private growth-equity commitment may be underwritten below a 15% base-case IRR; this
is the minimum return the mandate demands before capital is considered investable.

events.quote:
Limit Threshold
Minimum underwritten IRR (hurdle
rate)
15% at entry, base case
Single-name concentration cap 10% of AUM, at cost, per portfolio company
Sector concentration cap
10% of AUM, at co

events.text: This cannot be assessed until the post-money valuation and cap

table are provided under NDA.

**Concentration check:** The Charter caps
single-name concentration at 10% of A
UM, at cost, and the sector bucket (Technology and Consumer combined for the private book) at 10% of AUM, at cost.

events.quote:
Limit Threshold
Minimum underwritten IRR (hurdle
rate)
15% at entry, base case
Single-name concentration cap 10% of AUM, at cost, per portfolio company
Sector concentration cap
10% of AUM, at co

events.text: These caps are calculated post-deal, inclusive of the proposed new ticket, against total fir
m AUM at the time of the screening decision.

events.quote:
Concentration limits
The 10% single-name and 10% sector caps are calculated post-deal, i.e. inclusive of the proposed new ticket, against total
firm AUM at the time of the screening decision.

events.text: The SAR 40M ticket is ~2% of ~SAR 2.0bn AUM (analyst calculation), clearing

the single-name cap. Sector-bucket headroom is indeterminate from the sources because the current private-book sector exposure is not provided.

## 4

. Key Strengths and Concerns

**Strengths**
- **Expansion economics.**
Net revenue retention of
118% (FY2025A) rising to 124% (FY2026E)

events.quote:
Metric FY2025A FY2026E
Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) SAR 18.2M SAR 29.5M
YoY ARR growth — 62%
Gross margin 68% 71%
Net revenue retention (NRR) 118% 124%
Logo count (active customers) 34 52
Mont

events.text: indicates net upsell within
the installed base.
Management reports blended gross-dollar-retention above 90%, with expansion driven primarily by additional fleet se
ats and API call-volume tiers as customers scale delivery volumes on the platform.

events.quote:
contract value
Quick-commerce networks 46% SAR 620K/yr
Enterprise shippers 33% SAR 540K/yr
Regional carriers 21% SAR 310K/yr
Management reports a blended gross-dollar-retention figure above 90%, w

events.text: - **Gross-margin trajectory.**
Gross margin of 68% (FY2025A) improving to 71% (FY2026E)

events.quote:
Metric FY2025A FY2026E
Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) SAR 18.2M SAR 29.5M
YoY ARR growth — 62%
Gross margin 68% 71%
Net revenue retention (NRR) 118% 124%
Logo count (active customers) 34 52
Mont

events.text: , consistent with a

software delivery model.
- **Founder domain fit.**
The CEO was previ
ously operations lead at a Riyadh-based quick-commerce platform, where he built the last-mile dispatch system that Darb's routing engine is desc
ended from.

events.quote:
FOUNDING TEAM
Faisal Al-Otaibi — Co-founder & CEO
Former operations lead at a Riyadh-based quickcommerce platform, where he built the last-mile
dispatch system that Darb's routing engine is descen

events.text:
**Concerns**
- **First institutional round.**
Darb's Series A was led by a regional seed/
early-stage fund with participation from two Saudi angel investors; the company has not previously raised from institutional growth-equity investors.

events.quote:
Existing investors
Darb's Series A was led by a regional seed/early-stage fund with participation from two Saudi angel investors; the company has
not previously raised from institutional growth-equi

events.text:
This implies governance and reporting infrastructure has not been tested at institutional standard.
- **Competitive w
edge is a build-vs-buy argument.**
The most direct competition is in-house tooling built by large
logistics operators and quick-commerce platforms themselves, rather than a single dominant third-party vendor; management's stated wedge is faster time-to-deploy than
an in-house build, plus a routing engine tuned for Saudi urban delivery density.

events.quote:
COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
Darb's most direct competition is in-house tooling built by large logistics operators and quick-commerce platforms themselves,
rather than a single dominant third-party vendor.

events.text: Customers with
scale and engineering capacity can insource, capping pricing power (

judgment).
- **Data quality.**
Figures are Darb's own reporting, unaudited, as
shared at the screening stage.

events.quote: Figures below are Darb's own reporting, unaudited, as shared at the screening stage.

and regional carriers 21% at SAR 310K

CUSTOMERS & UNIT ECONOMICS
Darb's customer base spans three segments: quick-commerce dark-store networks (the largest and fastest-growing segment by
revenue), enterprise shippers with outsourced las

ick-commerce would disproportionately affect revenue |

**Analyst judgment:** the ~2.0x burn
multiple and NRR above 118% would, on the screening data
alone, be consistent with a fundable growth profile; this view would change materially if audited financials rev
ise ARR or margins, or if the entry valuation implies a base-case IRR below the

15% benchmark.

---

## 6. Company and Market Overview
Darb builds fleet- and route-optimization software for last-mile and middle-
mile logistics operators in the Kingdom; the platform sits between shippers and carrier fleets, providing route planning, real-time tracking, proof-of-delivery,
and carrier-performance analytics through a single dashboard and API layer.

events.quote:
COMPANY PROFILE
Darb builds fleet- and route-optimization software for last-mile and middle-mile logistics operators in the Kingdom. The
platform sits between shippers and carrier fleets, providing

events.text: Customers are mid-to-large logistics operators,
quick-commerce dark-store networks, and enterprise shippers who outsource last-mile delivery.

events.quote:
Darb's customers are mid-to-large logistics operators, quick-commerce dark-store networks, and enterprise shippers who
outsource last-mile delivery.

events.text: The product comprises a
Route Optimization Engine for dynamic routing across driver fleets, a F
leet Ops Dashboard for live tracking, proof-of-delivery and exception management, a Carrier API integration layer for third-party carrier networks and quick-
commerce platforms, and an Analytics Suite for delivery SLA, cost-per-order, and carrier-performance reporting.

events.quote:
Product
Route Optimization Engine — dynamic routing across driver fleets, reducing per-order delivery cost.
Fleet Ops Dashboard — live tracking, proof-of-delivery, exception management.
Carrier API

events.text: On the market,
Darb operates against rapid growth in Saudi e-commerce and quick-commerce delivery volumes and the logistics-
digitization push under Vision 2030's transport and logistics pillar; management frames the opportunity as software infrastructure for a delivery market growing faster than the f
leets serving it can scale manually.

events.quote:
Market
Darb operates against the backdrop of rapid growth in Saudi e-commerce and quick-commerce delivery volumes, and the
broader push toward logistics-sector digitization under Vision 2030's trans

events.text: Note: the pack does not quantify TAM, market share, or the growth rate

of the addressable market; these should be sourced in diligence.

---

## 8. Compliance Screen

The Charter requires a m
andatory compliance pre-screen resting on
two quantitative ratios plus a qualitative business-
activity test.

events.quote:
The methodology mirrors the AAOIFI / Tadawul screening approach used across the Saudi market and rests on two
quantitative ratios plus a qualitative business-activity test.

events.text: - **Business-activity test:** logistics SaaS is not among the excluded activities (
conventional banking/insurance, alcohol, gambling, conventional financial services, and similarly excluded activities

events.quote:
Test Threshold
Business activity
Core activity must be Shariah-permissible (excludes conventional banking/insurance, alcohol,
gambling, conventional financial services, and similarly excluded activ

events.text: ). Preliminary pass

on the qualitative test.
- **Interest-bearing debt ratio:** the thre
shold is
interest-bearing debt below 33% of the post-money valuation used at screening.

events.quote:
Test Threshold
Business activity
Core activity must be Shariah-permissible (excludes conventional banking/insurance, alcohol,
gambling, conventional financial services, and similarly excluded activ

events.text:
Not testable: neither debt balances nor a post-money valuation are in the pack.
- **Non-permissible income rat
io:** the threshold is
income from non-permissible sources below 5% of total revenue.

events.quote:
Test Threshold
Business activity
Core activity must be Shariah-permissible (excludes conventional banking/insurance, alcohol,
gambling, conventional financial services, and similarly excluded activ

events.text: No non-permissible income

is indicated for a SaaS revenue base, but this is not evidenced and must be confirmed.

**Red-flag checks
:** the Charter requires
sanctions screening of founders, directors and beneficial ow
ners; conflicts-of-interest cross-checks; related-party exposure review; and litigation and regulatory-history review, with a clean pass on all four required before an
opportunity may advance to analysis.

events.quote:
Red-flag & exclusion checks
Alongside the Shariah pre-screen, every opportunity is run through a standard set of red-flag checks before it may advance
past screening:
Sanctions screening — founders

events.text: None of the four is evidenced in the screening pack; all are outstanding.
Under the Charter,
a company failing either quantitative ratio is flagged and referred to the Committee for a
purification-and-waiver discussion rather than an automatic exclusion.

events.quote:
A company failing either quantitative ratio is flagged and
referred to the Committee for a purification-and-waiver discussion rather than an automatic exclusion, consistent with
standard AAOIFI prac

events.text:

advisory output is non-decisional; the investment decision rests with the Investment Committee, which votes to approve, def
er, or reject.*
